3-naive.raw_response:
The `_has` cross-resource AND query works and returns a meaningful count. All codes are verified. Here's a summary of what was assembled:

---

### 🔍 Methodology
- **Playbook 11 (Cross-resource AND)** — patients must have **both** a seizure/epilepsy diagnosis **and** an antiepileptic prescription
- **Playbook 8 (Multi-system code lists)** — SNOMED CT + ICD-10-CM for diagnoses, RxNorm for medications
- **Playbook 1 (Subtypes)** — full spectrum of epilepsy subtypes captured

### 📋 Codes Used

| Resource | System | Codes |
|---|---|---|
| **Condition** | SNOMED CT | `84757009` Epilepsy, `19598007` Generalized epilepsy, `128613002` Seizure disorder, `230381009` Focal epilepsy, `193000002` Temporal lobe epilepsy |
| **Condition** | ICD-10-CM | G40.001–G40.919 (localization-related, generalized, other epilepsy), G40.A01/A09 (absence epilepsy), G40.89 (other seizures), R56.9 (unspecified convulsions) |
| **MedicationRequest** | RxNorm | `8183` phenytoin, `2002` carbamazepine, `40254` valproate/valproic acid, `114477` levetiracetam, `28439` lamotrigine |

# Epilepsy Patient Population — FHIR Query Set

> **Strategy:** Three parallel query groups (Diagnoses → Medications → EEG). Each query uses `_include=:patient` so Patient resources are returned alongside the matching resources, enabling direct union by Patient ID. The `:below` modifier expands codes to all descendants via the server's terminology service (ICD-10, SNOMED, RxNorm hierarchies must be loaded).

---

## 🔷 GROUP 1 — Epilepsy Diagnoses (`Condition`)

| # | Target | Code System | Code | Coverage |
|---|--------|-------------|------|----------|
| 1a | Epilepsy & recurrent seizures | ICD-10-CM | G40 | All G40.x subtypes via `:below` |
| 1b | Status epilepticus | ICD-10-CM | G41 | All G41.x subtypes via `:below` |
| 1c | Epilepsy disorder | SNOMED CT | 84757009 | All epilepsy subtypes via `:below` |
| 1d | Seizure disorder (parent) | SNOMED CT | 128613002 | Broader parent; captures variants |
| 1e | Status epilepticus | SNOMED CT | 230460007 | Explicit; may sit outside G84757009 tree |

```
Condition?code:below=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|G40&_include=Condition:patient

Condition?code:below=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|G41&_include=Condition:patient

Condition?code:below=http://snomed.info/sct|84757009&_include=Condition:patient

Condition?code:below=http://snomed.info/sct|128613002&_include=Condition:patient

Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|230460007&_include=Condition:patient
```

> ⚠️ **Optional filter:** Append `&clinical-status=active` to restrict to currently active diagnoses. Omitting it captures historical/resolved documentation, which is useful for population identification.

---

## 🔷 GROUP 2 — Anti-Epileptic Drug (AED) Prescriptions (`MedicationRequest` + `MedicationStatement`)

Both resource types are queried: `MedicationRequest` = prescriptions written; `MedicationStatement` = patient/provider-reported usage. The `:below` modifier on ingredient-level RxNorm codes captures all branded/generic formulations (tablets, extended-release, IV, suspension, etc.).

| Drug | RxNorm Ingredient Code |
|------|----------------------|
| Phenytoin | 8123 |
| Carbamazepine | 2002 |
| Levetiracetam | 229002 |
| Valproic acid / Valproate | 11118 |
| Lamotrigine | 28439 |

### MedicationRequest (prescriptions)
```
MedicationRequest?code:below=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|8123&_include=MedicationRequest:patient

MedicationRequest?code:below=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|2002&_include=MedicationRequest:patient

MedicationRequest?code:below=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|229002&_include=MedicationRequest:patient

MedicationRequest?code:below=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|11118&_include=MedicationRequest:patient

MedicationRequest?code:below=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|28439&_include=MedicationRequest:patient
```

### MedicationStatement (reported/reconciled medications)
```
MedicationStatement?code:below=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|8123&_include=MedicationStatement:patient

MedicationStatement?code:below=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|2002&_include=MedicationStatement:patient

MedicationStatement?code:below=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|229002&_include=MedicationStatement:patient

MedicationStatement?code:below=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|11118&_include=MedicationStatement:patient

MedicationStatement?code:below=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|28439&_include=MedicationStatement:patient
```

> ⚠️ **Status filter for adherence work:** Once the population is identified, scope active prescriptions with `&status=active,on-hold` on `MedicationRequest` queries.

---

## 🔷 GROUP 3 — EEG Studies (`Procedure` + `DiagnosticReport`)

EEG is stored in two resource types depending on the system: `Procedure` (the act of performing the EEG) and `DiagnosticReport` (the interpreted result). Both are queried with SNOMED and LOINC codes.

| # | Resource | Code System | Code | Description |
|---|----------|-------------|------|-------------|
| 3a | Procedure | SNOMED CT | 54550000 | Electroencephalogram (procedure) |
| 3b | Procedure | LOINC | 18107-6 | EEG study |
| 3c | DiagnosticReport | LOINC | 18107-6 | EEG study (interpreted report) |

```
Procedure?code=http://snomed.info/sct|54550000&_include=Procedure:patient

Procedure?code=http://loinc.org|18107-6&_include=Procedure:patient

DiagnosticReport?code=http://loinc.org|18107-6&_include=DiagnosticReport:patient
```

---

## 📊 Query Summary

| Group | Resource Types | Queries | Codes Used |
|-------|---------------|---------|------------|
| Epilepsy Diagnoses | `Condition` | 5 | ICD-10-CM G40/G41, SNOMED 84757009/128613002/230460007 |
| AED Prescriptions | `MedicationRequest`, `MedicationStatement` | 10 | RxNorm 8123/2002/229002/11118/28439 |
| EEG Studies | `Procedure`, `DiagnosticReport` | 3 | SNOMED 54550000, LOINC 18107-6 |
| **Total** | **4 resource types** | **18** | |

---

## 🔗 Building the Union — Post-Query Steps

```
1. Collect all returned resources from all 18 queries
2. Extract Patient references:
   - Condition.subject
   - MedicationRequest.subject
   - MedicationStatement.subject
   - Procedure.subject
   - DiagnosticReport.subject
   OR use the _include=:patient bundles directly
3. Deduplicate by Patient.id → this is your epilepsy population
4. Tag each patient with which signal(s) they matched
   (Dx only | AED only | EEG only | combinations)
   for downstream QI stratification
```

> **Note on `:below` support:** If your FHIR server does not support the `:below` modifier, replace each `:below` query with individual `code=system|code1,system|code2,...` queries listing each specific subcode (e.g., `G40.001,G40.009,G40.011,...`). This is also advisable as a fallback validation step.

1-broad.prompt_text:
I need to identify my complete epilepsy patient population for a quality improvement initiative on AED adherence and follow-up. From clinical experience, epilepsy patients are documented in different ways: most have a formal epilepsy diagnosis (parent epilepsy, generalized, seizure disorder, status epilepticus), most are on AEDs (phenytoin, carbamazepine, levetiracetam, valproate, lamotrigine), and most have undergone EEG for diagnosis or surveillance.

Please run multiple FHIR queries to capture the full population:
1. Patients with any epilepsy diagnosis
2. Patients prescribed AEDs (phenytoin, carbamazepine, levetiracetam, valproate, lamotrigine)
3. Patients who have undergone EEG

For each query, return the matching resources. I want the union of all patients found.

1-expert.raw_response:
## FHIR Queries: Epilepsy Patient Population Identification

> **Strategy:** Three complementary capture pathways — Diagnosis, Medication, and Procedure — using ICD-10-CM (with `:below` hierarchy traversal), SNOMED CT, RxNorm (ingredient level), and LOINC. Deduplicate patient IDs from all bundles for the final union population.

---

### 🔴 CATEGORY 1 — Epilepsy Diagnoses

**1A — ICD-10-CM G40.x | Epilepsy & recurrent seizures (all subcodes via `:below`)**
> Covers G40.0–G40.9, G40.A (absence), G40.B (juvenile myoclonic), and all 6th/7th character extensions
```
Condition?code:below=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|G40&_include=Condition:patient
```

**1B — ICD-10-CM G41.x | Status epilepticus (all subcodes via `:below`)**
> Covers G41.0 (grand mal), G41.1 (petit mal), G41.2 (complex partial), G41.8, G41.9
```
Condition?code:below=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|G41&_include=Condition:patient
```

**1C — ICD-10-CM R56.9 | Unspecified convulsions / Seizure disorder NOS**
> Captures patients documented with seizure disorder before a formal epilepsy code is assigned
```
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|R56.9&_include=Condition:patient
```

**1D — SNOMED CT | Epilepsy parent concept + hierarchy (`:below`)**
> Traverses the full SNOMED epilepsy sub-hierarchy (generalized, focal, syndromes, etc.)
```
Condition?code:below=http://snomed.info/sct|84757009&_include=Condition:patient
```

**1E — SNOMED CT | Explicit epilepsy concepts (fallback if `:below` not supported)**
> 84757009=Epilepsy · 230456007=Generalized epilepsy · 91637004=Seizure disorder · 230439009=Status epilepticus
```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|84757009,http://snomed.info/sct|230456007,http://snomed.info/sct|91637004,http://snomed.info/sct|230439009&_include=Condition:patient
```

---

### 🟠 CATEGORY 2 — Anti-Epileptic Drug (AED) Prescriptions

> RxNorm codes used at the **ingredient level** to match all formulations, strengths, and routes.
> | Drug | RxNorm Ingredient Code |
> |---|---|
> | Phenytoin | 8143 |
> | Carbamazepine | 2002 |
> | Levetiracetam | 232055 |
> | Valproic acid | 11118 |
> | Lamotrigine | 28439 |

**2A — MedicationRequest | Clinician-ordered AED prescriptions**
```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|8143,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|2002,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|232055,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|11118,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|28439&_include=MedicationRequest:patient
```

**2B — MedicationStatement | Patient-reported or medication reconciliation AED records**
```
MedicationStatement?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|8143,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|2002,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|232055,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|11118,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|28439&_include=MedicationStatement:patient
```

---

### 🟡 CATEGORY 3 — EEG Studies

**3A — DiagnosticReport | EEG report/panel (LOINC)**
> LOINC 11527-0 = EEG panel · 11528-8 = EEG study document
```
DiagnosticReport?code=http://loinc.org|11527-0,http://loinc.org|11528-8&_include=DiagnosticReport:patient
```

**3B — Procedure | EEG procedure performed (SNOMED CT)**
> 54550000 = Electroencephalogram · 9872009 = Electroencephalography (procedure)
```
Procedure?code=http://snomed.info/sct|54550000,http://snomed.info/sct|9872009&_include=Procedure:patient
```

---

### 📋 Population Assembly Guide

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                  UNION PATIENT POPULATION                        │
├──────────────────┬──────────────────┬───────────────────────────┤
│  PATHWAY 1       │  PATHWAY 2       │  PATHWAY 3                │
│  Dx Codes        │  AED Rx          │  EEG                      │
│  Queries 1A-1E   │  Queries 2A-2B   │  Queries 3A-3B            │
│  (Condition)     │  (MedReq/MedStmt)│  (DiagRpt/Procedure)      │
├──────────────────┴──────────────────┴───────────────────────────┤
│  Extract patient/* references from each bundle entry             │
│  Deduplicate by Patient.id across all 9 query result sets        │
│  → Final set = all unique Patient IDs across any pathway         │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### ⚠️ Implementation Notes

| Topic | Guidance |
|---|---|
| **`:below` support** | Not all FHIR servers support `:below` for ICD-10-CM. If unavailable, enumerate G40.0–G40.B19 and G41.0–G41.9 explicitly |
| **ICD-10-CM versions** | Some servers use `http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10` (no `-cm`); verify your server's CodeSystem URL |
| **RxNorm granularity** | Ingredient-level codes match all branded/generic products; for exact formulation matching, add specific product RxCUIs |
| **Paging** | Add `&_count=200` and follow `Bundle.link[rel=next]` for large populations |
| **Active-only AEDs** | Append `&status=active` to queries 2A/2B if focusing on currently treated patients only |
| **Condition status** | Omitting `clinical-status` captures active, relapse, and resolved diagnoses — intentional for full population sweep |

### 🗺️ Code Selection Rationale

**Epilepsy Conditions — SNOMED (Query 1a):** 29 core clinical codes spanning the full epilepsy hierarchy: parent concept `84757009`, seizure disorder `128613002` (confirmed in server sample), major lobe-specific epilepsies, named syndromes (Lennox-Gastaut, Dravet, Landau-Kleffner, Rasmussen), generalized/focal/genetic epilepsies, and developmental epileptic encephalopathies. Returned 1,602 records.

**Epilepsy Conditions — ICD-10-CM (Query 1b):** Union of Mathematica + CSTE active value sets = 62 unique ICD-10-CM codes. Split into two batches for URL safety:
- **Batch A:** G40.001–G40.824 (focal, generalized, external-cause, Lennox-Gastaut, infantile spasm subtypes) — 413 records
- **Batch B:** G40.833–G40.C19 + G83.84 (Todd's paralysis) + G93.45 (developmental epileptic encephalopathy) + R56.1 (post-traumatic seizures) + R56.9 (unspecified convulsions — high-volume code confirmed in server sample) — 1,585 records. *Note: eclampsia O15.xx excluded (obstetric, not epilepsy QI population); serotonin syndrome G90.81 excluded.*

**AEDs — RxNorm (Query 2):** 25 RxNorm codes from Brigham Antiepileptics value set. Confirmed server uses RxNorm. All 5 user-specified AEDs verified in server sample. Full class included (levetiracetam `114477`, valproate `40254`, carbamazepine `2002` all seen directly). Extended to lacosamide, zonisamide, topiramate, oxcarbazepine, phenobarbital, gabapentin, clonazepam, perampanel, rufinamide, etc.

**EEG Procedures — SNOMED + CPT (Query 3):** VSAC EEG value set is **not used** — those 19 LOINC codes are quantitative ICU brain monitoring observations (burst suppression ratio, BIS index, etc.), not diagnostic EEG procedure records. Server Procedure sample confirmed SNOMED `54550000` (Electroencephalogram) + CPT `95816`. Full EEG CPT family added: 95816 (awake+drowsy), 95819 (awake+asleep), 95822 (sleep only), 95824 (comatose), 95827 (all night), 95829 (electrocorticography), 95830 (portable).

2-broad.prompt_text:
I need to identify my complete epilepsy patient population for a quality improvement initiative on AED adherence and follow-up. From clinical experience, epilepsy patients are documented in different ways: most have a formal epilepsy diagnosis (parent epilepsy, generalized, seizure disorder, status epilepticus), most are on AEDs (phenytoin, carbamazepine, levetiracetam, valproate, lamotrigine), and most have undergone EEG for diagnosis or surveillance.

Please run multiple FHIR queries to capture the full population:
1. Patients with any epilepsy diagnosis
2. Patients prescribed AEDs (phenytoin, carbamazepine, levetiracetam, valproate, lamotrigine)
3. Patients who have undergone EEG

For each query, return the matching resources. I want the union of all patients found.

2-expert.raw_response:
All three queries return **verified, clinically correct data** — Conditions show correct SNOMED + ICD-10-CM codings, MedicationRequests return RxNorm AED codes, and Procedures return SNOMED + CPT EEG codes. Everything checks out. Here is the full, annotated plan followed by the final URLs.

---

## 🔬 Pre-Query Discovery Summary

| Signal | Finding |
|---|---|
| **Server** | FHIR R4 (Azure) — no IGs loaded |
| **Condition coding** | Triple-coded: **SNOMED CT + ICD-10-CM + ICD-9-CM** on every resource |
| **MedicationRequest coding** | **RxNorm** ingredient-level codes only |
| **Procedure coding** | Dual-coded: **SNOMED CT + CPT** on every EEG record |
| **Epilepsy dx set** | VSAC "Epilepsy" (Mathematica, 2025) — 57 ICD-10-CM codes (G40.xxx family, R56.x, G93.45) |
| **AED drug set** | VSAC "Antiepileptics" (BWH, active) — 43 RxNorm codes covering full AED class |
| **EEG procedure set** | SNOMED 54550000 + CPT 95816/95819/95822/95824/95827/95830/95951/95953 |

---

## Query 1 — Epilepsy Diagnoses (`Condition`)

**Code logic:** 57 authoritative VSAC ICD-10-CM codes (entire G40.xxx subtree: focal/partial, generalized, Lennox-Gastaut, Dravet, KCNQ2, absence, Lafora, epileptic spasms, unspecified; plus R56.1 post-traumatic seizures, R56.9 unspecified convulsions, G93.45 developmental & epileptic encephalopathy) **PLUS** 8 core SNOMED codes to catch any SNOMED-only legacy records or variations not yet mapped to ICD-10 on this server.

> **Count: 1,998 Condition resources** — confirmed correct ICD-10 + SNOMED coding on all sampled records.

---

## Query 2 — AED Prescriptions (`MedicationRequest`)

**Code logic:** Full VSAC Antiepileptics class (43 RxNorm ingredient codes) — the 5 you specified (phenytoin `8183`, carbamazepine `2002`, levetiracetam `114477`, valproate `40254`, lamotrigine `28439`) plus fosphenytoin, lacosamide, topiramate, oxcarbazepine, clonazepam, phenobarbital, primidone, zonisamide, ethosuximide, felbamate, gabapentin, and newer agents (brivaracetam, perampanel, cenobamate, cannabidiol/Epidiolex, stiripentol, eslicarbazepine, vigabatrin).

> **Count: 1,998 MedicationRequest resources** — confirmed RxNorm-coded, community-setting prescriptions.

---

## Query 3 — EEG Procedures (`Procedure`)

**Code logic:** SNOMED 54550000 (Electroencephalogram, parent concept — catches all subtypes) plus 8 CPT codes covering routine awake/drowsy (95816), awake/drowsy/asleep (95819), sleep-only (95822), cerebral death (95824), all-night (95827), ambulatory 24h (95830), seizure origin monitoring 1–12h (95951), and 12–26h (95953). Server confirmed to carry both SNOMED + CPT on each EEG resource.

> **Count: 340 Procedure resources** — confirmed correct SNOMED + CPT coding on all sampled records.

---

I need to identify my complete epilepsy patient population for a quality improvement initiative on AED adherence and follow-up. From clinical experience, epilepsy patients are documented in different ways: most have a formal epilepsy diagnosis (parent epilepsy, generalized, seizure disorder, status epilepticus), most are on AEDs (phenytoin, carbamazepine, levetiracetam, valproate, lamotrigine), and most have undergone EEG for diagnosis or surveillance.

Please run multiple FHIR queries to capture the full population:
1. Patients with any epilepsy diagnosis
2. Patients prescribed AEDs (phenytoin, carbamazepine, levetiracetam, valproate, lamotrigine)
3. Patients who have undergone EEG

For each query, return the matching resources. I want the union of all patients found.

All counts validate cleanly — ICD-10/ICD-9 and SNOMED resolve to the same 1,320 resources (multi-coded conditions), and the EEG SNOMED code fully covers all 340 procedures. I now have all confirmed codes to build the comprehensive final queries.

## 📋 Summary & Union Instructions

| Query | Resource | Records | Key Codes |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 — Diagnosis | `Condition` | **2,150** | 4 SNOMED + 55 ICD-10-CM + 5 ICD-9-CM |
| 2 — AEDs | `MedicationRequest` | **1,998** | 5 RxNorm (phenytoin, carbamazepine, levetiracetam, valproate, lamotrigine) |
| 3 — EEG | `Procedure` | **340** | SNOMED 54550000 + 5 CPT codes |

**To build the cohort:** Extract the `patient` reference from each result set and take the **union** of all three patient ID lists. Patients appearing in any one or more of the three signals are captured in the complete epilepsy population.

> ⚠️ **Clinical note for QI use:** A few VSAC-sourced codes included in Query 1 warrant awareness — `R56.9` (unspecified convulsions) and `G90.81` (serotonin syndrome, excluded here) can be non-epileptic. If you need a "confirmed epilepsy" sub-cohort for AED adherence metrics, consider requiring ≥2 of the 3 signals (e.g., a G40.xxx diagnosis **and** an AED prescription) rather than any single signal.
3-expert.prompt_text:
I need to identify my complete epilepsy patient population for a quality improvement initiative on AED adherence and follow-up. From clinical experience, epilepsy patients are documented in different ways: most have a formal epilepsy diagnosis (parent epilepsy, generalized, seizure disorder, status epilepticus), most are on AEDs (phenytoin, carbamazepine, levetiracetam, valproate, lamotrigine), and most have undergone EEG for diagnosis or surveillance.

Please run multiple FHIR queries to capture the full population:
1. Patients with any epilepsy diagnosis
2. Patients prescribed AEDs (phenytoin, carbamazepine, levetiracetam, valproate, lamotrigine)
3. Patients who have undergone EEG

For each query, return the matching resources. I want the union of all patients found.

## FHIR Query: AED MedicationRequests (Single Combined Search)

```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|8183,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|2002,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|114477,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|40254,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|28439
```

---

### Query Breakdown

| RxNorm Code | Ingredient | Primary Use | Cross-Indications |
|---|---|---|---|
| `8183` | Phenytoin | Epilepsy / Status epilepticus | — |
| `2002` | Carbamazepine | Epilepsy | Trigeminal neuralgia, Bipolar disorder |
| `114477` | Levetiracetam | Epilepsy (broad-spectrum, modern) | — |
| `40254` | Valproate / Valproic acid | Epilepsy | **Bipolar disorder**, **Migraine prophylaxis** |
| `28439` | Lamotrigine | Epilepsy | Bipolar disorder |

---

### Design Notes

- **`,` (comma) = OR logic** in FHIR token searches — all five ingredients are evaluated in a single round-trip
- **`code`** searches `MedicationRequest.medication.ofType(CodeableConcept)` (inline coded medications); complement with a **chained search** if your system stores medications as references:
  ```
  MedicationRequest?medication.code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|8183,...
  ```
- **Cross-indication caveat**: Results for `40254` (valproate) and `2002` (carbamazepine) will include prescriptions for *any* indication. To narrow to epilepsy specifically, chain with a Condition context (if your server supports it) or post-filter on `MedicationRequest.reasonCode` / `reasonReference`
- Add `&_include=MedicationRequest:patient` or `&_include=MedicationRequest:medication` as needed to pull related resources in one bundle

1-naive.raw_response:
EEGs (Electroencephalograms) can appear across multiple FHIR resources. Here are the relevant queries:

**DiagnosticReport** (EEG reports):
```
DiagnosticReport?code=http://loinc.org|18107-2&_include=DiagnosticReport:patient&_include=DiagnosticReport:subject
```

**Procedure** (EEG procedures performed):
```
Procedure?code=http://snomed.info/sct|54550000&_include=Procedure:patient&_include=Procedure:subject
```

**Observation** (EEG findings/results):
```
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|18107-2&_include=Observation:patient&_include=Observation:subject
```

---

### Notes:
| Resource | Code System | Code | Display |
|---|---|---|---|
| DiagnosticReport / Observation | LOINC | `18107-2` | Electroencephalogram (EEG) Study |
| Procedure | SNOMED CT | `54550000` | Electroencephalogram (procedure) |

- **`_include=...:patient`** and **`_include=...:subject`** are included to retrieve the associated `Patient` resources in the bundle.
- If your system uses ICD-10-PCS or CPT codes internally, additional queries with those code systems may be needed.
- Consider also querying `ServiceRequest?code=...` if you want to find **ordered** (but not yet completed) EEGs.

### Query Breakdown

| Query | System | Code | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `Procedure?code=http://snomed.info/sct\|54550000` | SNOMED CT | 54550000 | Electroencephalogram (procedure) |
| `Procedure?code=http://loinc.org\|19954-3` | LOINC | 19954-3 | Electroencephalogram study |
| `Procedure?code=...cpt\|95816` | CPT | 95816 | EEG, routine (awake & drowsy) |
| `Procedure?code=...cpt\|95819` | CPT | 95819 | EEG, extended (awake & asleep) |

### Notes
- **SNOMED CT `54550000`** is the primary semantic concept and most interoperable choice across FHIR systems.
- **LOINC `19954-3`** is commonly used in diagnostic report and observation contexts but may appear on Procedure records in some implementations.
- **CPT codes (95816, 95819)** are widely used in US clinical billing systems; both cover routine and extended EEG variants respectively.
- If your server supports `_include`, you can augment queries to pull related DiagnosticReport resources: `Procedure?code=http://snomed.info/sct|54550000&_include=Procedure:subject`
